description_long: This is a traditional hand axe featuring a robust, slightly curved wooden handle with a natural grain and a darkened, worn finish, suggesting frequent use. The handle is thickest at the base and tapers gently toward the head, providing a comfortable grip. The axe head is forged from dark, weathered metal, with a broad, wedge-shaped blade and a flat poll opposite the cutting edge. The blade has a single, slightly convex edge, and the head is securely mounted onto the handle using a traditional wedged fitting. There are no visible decorative elements or branding, giving the tool a utilitarian, workmanlike appearance. The overall design is practical and suited for chopping or splitting wood in urban environments such as parks, gardens, or garages.
description: A traditional hand axe with a curved wooden handle and a broad, dark metal blade, showing signs of wear and practical use.
description_view_0: The axe is seen from the top of the handle, showing the rounded wooden end and a glimpse of the metal head.
description_view_1: A full side profile reveals the curved wooden handle and the broad, wedge-shaped metal blade.
description_view_2: The axe is viewed from the top of the blade, highlighting the narrow edge and the alignment of the handle with the head.
description_view_3: The opposite side profile displays the same curved handle and blade, with visible wood grain and metal texture.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with a natural grain, oval cross-section, thick at the base, tapers toward the head, ergonomic grip.
attribute_blade_design: Single broad wedge-shaped steel blade with a convex edge and flat poll, no beard, utilitarian design.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged fitting securing the blade to the wooden handle.
